description_long: This is a white and black wireless game controller, most likely designed for use with an Xbox gaming console. The front face features two analog sticks, a directional pad, four colored action buttons labeled Y, X, B, and A, and a central Xbox logo button. The top edge houses two pairs of shoulder buttons and triggers, while the back is smooth with a battery compartment cover. The controller is ergonomically shaped with contoured grips for comfortable handheld use. The materials appear to be primarily plastic with some rubberized elements for grip and tactile feedback. This object is typically used for interactive entertainment and gaming in home or urban recreational environments.
description: A white and black wireless Xbox-style game controller with colored buttons, analog sticks, and ergonomic grips. Designed for handheld gaming and interactive use.
description_view_0: Front view showing analog sticks, D-pad, colored action buttons, and the Xbox logo button.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the contoured grip and the shoulder buttons and triggers on the top edge.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the smooth back and battery compartment cover.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the other grip and the profile of the shoulder buttons and triggers.
